1. Kramer and Lopez each review fewer of the plays than Megregian.

2. Neither Lopez nor Megregian reviews any play Jiang reviews.

So Jiang's plays are only possibly reviewed by Kramer and O'Neill? Actually the condition says "Neither Lopez nor Megregian reviews any play Jiang reviews." 3. Kramer and O'Neill both review Tamerlane.

4. Exactly two of the students review exactly the same play or plays as each other.
   This means there is exactly one pair of students who have identical sets of plays. All other students have distinct sets (or at least not exactly the same as any other).
